## Introduction

This is the first article published on my technical blog.

The purpose of this post is to verify that Quarto correctly renders:

- mathematical equations;
- Python code;
- Jupyter output;
- figures;
- links to external repositories.

## A mathematical example

Consider the mean squared error:

$$
\mathcal{L}(\theta)
=
\frac{1}{N}
\sum_{i=1}^{N}
\left(f_\theta(x_i)-y_i\right)^2
$$ {#eq-mse}

Equation @eq-mse measures the average squared difference between predictions and targets.

## A Python example

::: {#bade00e3 .cell execution_count=1}
``` {.python .cell-code}
import numpy as np 
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

x = np.linspace(-3, 3, 200) 
y = x**2

plt.plot(x, y) 
plt.xlabel("x") 
plt.ylabel("x²") 
plt.title("Quadratic function") 
plt.show()
```
